name: Castillete de Fayón y Mausoleo de Fabara — circular desde Nonaspe
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Nonaspe navigate a diverse landscape at the confluence of the Matarraña and Algars rivers, which flow into the Ebro. The terrain features fertile plains with fruit trees and olive groves, transitioning to horizons of steppe-like mountains. This region offers a mix of gradients, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging climbs in elevated areas. The Matarraña river is known for its well-preserved fluvial ecosystems and scenic meanders. Best touring cycling routes around Nonaspe The most popular touring cycling route is Castillete de Fayón y Mausoleo de Fabara — circular desde Nonaspe, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 36 minutes to complete, offering views of historical sites and varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gandesa loop from la Pobla de Massaluca, a difficult 28.7 miles (46.2 km) path that traverses diverse terrain with significant elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Roman Mausoleum of Fabara loop from Fabara / Favara, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail leading through agricultural lands and historical points, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Nonaspe is defined by river meanders, olive groves, and steppe mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to difficult climbs. The routes in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 80 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nonaspe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nonaspe?
A: There are 18 touring cycling routes available around Nonaspe, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These routes navigate a diverse landscape at the confluence of the Matarraña and Algars rivers, which flow into the Ebro.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nonaspe?
A: Nonaspe's touring cycling routes feature a compelling mix of landscapes. You'll find fertile plains with fruit trees and olive groves, transitioning to horizons of steppe-like mountains. This provides a range of gradients, from gentle riverside paths along the Matarraña and Algars rivers to more challenging climbs in elevated areas.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Nonaspe?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For an easy option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40002795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Badón loop from Fayón / Faió&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 8.3 km with a moderate elevation gain, making it a good choice for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Nonaspe's routes offer a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore the confluence of the Matarraña and Algars rivers, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4613070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Mausoleum of Fabara&lt;/a&gt;, or pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its well-preserved Matarraña river ecosystems and scenic meanders, often bordered by orchards.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Nonaspe?
A: Yes, many routes in Nonaspe are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993711285/castillete-de-fayon-y-mausoleo-de-fabara-circular-desde-nonaspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillete de Fayón y Mausoleo de Fabara — circular desde Nonaspe&lt;/a&gt;, a 38.6 km trail that offers views of historical sites and varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Nonaspe?
A: While specific weather conditions vary, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. These seasons offer milder temperatures, which are more comfortable for longer rides, allowing you to fully enjoy the fertile plains, river meanders, and steppe mountains without extreme heat or cold.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Riba-roja Reservoir?
A: Yes, the tail end of the Riba-roja reservoir, where the Matarraña enters, forms a beautiful natural lake rich in birdlife and riparian forests. While not directly listed in the provided routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a significant natural highlight in the area, and routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mountain Peak – Santuario de la Fontcalda loop from la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt; offer views of the wider landscape which includes such features.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Nonaspe?
A: The touring cycling routes in Nonaspe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from gentle riverside paths to challenging climbs, and the scenic beauty of the river confluences and historical sites like the Roman Mausoleum of Fabara.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Nonaspe?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided for all routes, the presence of easy, gentle riverside paths suggests options suitable for families. The varied terrain allows for choices that cater to different fitness levels, making it possible to find routes that are enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the touring cycling routes?
A: Nonaspe is a village with local amenities, and many routes pass through or near other small towns like Fabara or Fayón. These villages typically offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where cyclists can rest, refuel, and enjoy local cuisine. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller communities.

Q: Is there a route that explores the Matarraña River in depth?
A: The Matarraña river is renowned for its well-preserved fluvial ecosystems and beautiful meanders. Many touring cycling routes in Nonaspe, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Mausoleum of Fabara loop from Fabara / Favara&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate sections along the river, allowing cyclists to experience its scenic beauty and the surrounding orchards.

Q: What is the most challenging touring cycling route in the area?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mountain Peak – Santuario de la Fontcalda loop from la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route covering 57 km with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38237337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gandesa loop from la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46.2 km long and also features considerable climbing.
tips: Located at the top of the hill, about seven kilometers from the town of Nonaspe, we find this building that was built as a watchtower during the third Carlist war in the 19th century to dominate the Matarraña valley.
Located in the town of Fabara, is the fortified Church of San Juan Bautista. A 15th century medieval church. Fortified and Gothic style.

On the outskirts of Fabara and on the banks of the Matarraña River, we find this Muselo. It is a Roman tomb that since ancient times has been known as Caseta dels Moros by the inhabitants of the area. It is quite possibly the best preserved monument of this type in the entire Peninsula.

Located in the Bajo Aragón-Caspe region and forming part of the Aragón Strip, we find the town of Nonaspe where the Castillo de la Encomienda (of medieval origin that is currently the town hall) and the Ethnological Museum stand out.

waypoints.name: Fortified Church of San Juan Bautista of Fabara
Castillo de la Encomienda de Nonaspe
